political.s: Wilson Sporting Goods does not have direct FEC or lobbying disclosures as a private company. Parent company Amer Sports went public in February 2026 but specific political donation data for Wilson subsidiary is not publicly disclosed.

charity.s: Wilson has extensive community engagement programs including $1.5 million partnership with YMCA, partnerships with Autism Speaks with minimum $150,000 annual donations, and collaborations with Good Sports and Pitch in for Baseball providing equipment to underserved youth.
charity.focus: Sports access for underserved youth, autism advocacy, equipment donation programs

environment.s: Wilson developed Gen Green collection using sustainable materials with 2x lower CO2 emissions than conventional products, uses recycled plastics and biobased sugarcane materials, and conducted waste audits at U.S. facilities to improve sustainability practices.

labor.s: 86% of Wilson employees report it as a great place to work versus 57% industry average. Company offers 16 weeks parental leave, three paid volunteer days annually, and health benefits. One 2017 OSHA fine of $65,000 for workplace safety violations at Ada, Ohio facility.

dei.s: Wilson has DEI rating of 3.4/5 stars from employees, equal to manufacturing sector average. Promotes female representation through Women Empowerment group and mentorship programs. Collaborates with Autism Speaks on Workplace Inclusion Now program.

animals.s: Wilson Sporting Goods produces sporting equipment (basketballs, footballs, rackets, etc.) and does not conduct animal testing. Products use leather materials from responsible suppliers like Horween Leather Company.

guns.s: Wilson Sporting Goods manufactures sporting equipment (balls, rackets, apparel) and does not make firearms. Company is distinct from Wilson Combat, an unrelated firearms manufacturer.

privacy.s: Wilson collects user data through website and Connected Football app with comprehensive privacy policy. Uses third-party cookies for advertising and analytics. Complies with CPRA and data protection laws but shares data with Amer Sports group entities.

execPay.s: Average executive compensation is $225,059 annually. CEO salary estimated at $265,697-$339,798. Salary range from $34,705 (retail associate) to $339,798 (CEO) indicates significant pay disparity.
